claim: Context degradation occurs when agents run long conversations and the context window fills up, pushing out important earlier decisions and degrading attention
actionable: For long-running agents, implement context compression and isolation strategies to prevent early decisions from being pushed out of the context window
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Guide for SKILL-Based Implementation

claim: Multi-agent systems with context isolation outperform single-agent pipelines: each agent gets a clean context window, preventing accumulated context and 'lost in the middle' effects
actionable: For complex tasks, architect multi-agent systems where each agent receives only relevant context, not accumulated state from previous agents
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Guide for SKILL-Based Implementation

claim: Progressive disclosure with skills: add debugging guides and edge-case handlers as separate files in a skill folder, and tell the agent about them - it reads them when needed
actionable: Organize agent skills with a main instruction file and separate support files for debugging/edge cases; declare all files in the skill description
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Guide for SKILL-Based Implementation
